content: {"analyses":[{"source_url":"https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC10253889/","source_title":"Effects of Intermittent Fasting on Regulation of Metabolic Homeostasis: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis","source_type":"journal_article","source_quality":0.88,"overall_relevance":0.92,"year":2023,"authors":[],"venue":"PMC / Nutrients","doi":"","atomic_facts":[{"statement":"The systematic review identified 64 reports eligible for qualitative analysis and 47 for the quantitative meta-analysis of intermittent fasting effects.","direct_quote":"Sixty-four reports met the eligibility criteria for the qualitative analysis and forty-seven for the quantitative analysis","fact_category":"methodology","relevance_score":0.82,"confidence":0.95,"perspective":"Methodological","entities":["systematic review","meta-analysis","intermittent fasting"]},{"statement":"Alternate-Day Fasting protocols produced greater beneficial effects on dysregulated metabolic conditions than Time-Restricted Fasting or Religious Fasting protocols.","direct_quote":"ADF protocols promoted the major beneficial effects in the improvement of dysregulated metabolic conditions in comparison with TRF and RF protocols","fact_category":"causal_link","relevance_score":0.95,"confidence":0.9,"perspective":"Scientific","entities":["Alternate-Day Fasting","Time-Restricted Fasting","Religious Fasting"]},{"statement":"Individuals with obesity and metabolic syndrome gain the greatest benefit from intermittent fasting via improvements in adiposity, lipid homeostasis, and blood pressure.","direct_quote":"obese and MetS individuals are the most benefited with the introduction of these interventions, through the improvement of adiposity, lipid homeostasis and blood pressure","fact_category":"causal_link","relevance_score":0.93,"confidence":0.9,"perspective":"Public_Health","entities":["obesity","metabolic syndrome","adiposity","blood pressure"]},{"statement":"The included population totaled 4052 participants comprising 2007 men and 1977 women, with gender undisclosed for 68 participants.","direct_quote":"the population comprised a total of n = 4052 participants of which n = 2007 were men and n = 1977 women","fact_category":"statistic","relevance_score":0.7,"confidence":0.95,"perspective":"Methodological","entities":["sample size","participants"]},{"statement":"Intermittent fasting may improve insulin sensitivity, reduce inflammation, and promote autophagy independently of weight loss via enhanced fatty acid oxidation.","direct_quote":"IF can improve insulin sensitivity, reduce inflammation and promote cellular repair and autophagy","fact_category":"causal_link","relevance_score":0.9,"confidence":0.85,"perspective":"Scientific","entities":["insulin sensitivity","inflammation","autophagy","fatty acid oxidation"]}],"evidence_summary":"Systematic review and meta-analysis of 64 studies showing IF benefits vary by protocol and basal metabolic status, with ADF and obese/MetS populations benefiting most."},{"source_url":"https://www.mdpi.com/2077-0383/12/11/3699","source_title":"Effects of intermittent fasting on regulation of metabolic homeostasis: a systematic review and meta-analysis in health and metabolic-related disorders","source_type":"journal_article","source_quality":0.85,"overall_relevance":0.9,"year":2023,"authors":[],"venue":"Journal of Clinical Medicine (MDPI)","doi":"","atomic_facts":[{"statement":"In Corley et al. 2018, a 5+2 ADF protocol in type 2 diabetes patients improved weight, HbA1c, fasting glucose, and quality of life but increased hypoglycaemia risk.","direct_quote":"IF in a 5 + 2 protocol (both consecutive and non-consecutive) increases the rate of hypoglycaemia in patients with T2D","fact_category":"causal_link","relevance_score":0.94,"confidence":0.9,"perspective":"Public_Health","entities":["type 2 diabetes","hypoglycaemia","HbA1c","5+2 ADF"]},{"statement":"In Hutchison et al. 2018, IF combined with 30% caloric restriction outperformed energy-matched continuous restriction on weight, fat mass, HOMA-IR, LDL, and triglycerides.","direct_quote":"IF combined with a 30% caloric restriction is better at improving weight, fat mass, HOMA-IR, fasting glucose, non-esterified fatty acids, total cholesterol, LDL-cholesterol and triglycerides","fact_category":"causal_link","relevance_score":0.92,"confidence":0.9,"perspective":"Scientific","entities":["HOMA-IR","LDL cholesterol","triglycerides","caloric restriction"]},{"statement":"Sundfor et al. 2018 found that over 365 days intermittent and continuous energy restriction produced similar weight loss and cardiovascular risk improvements but IF triggered more pronounced hunger.","direct_quote":"Both intermittent and continuous energy restriction promoted similar weight loss, maintenance and improvement in cardiovascular risk factors after one year","fact_category":"statistic","relevance_score":0.88,"confidence":0.9,"perspective":"Scientific","entities":["energy restriction","weight loss","cardiovascular risk","hunger"]},{"statement":"Kunduraci et al. 2020 reported that a 16+8 TRF protocol in metabolic syndrome patients significantly improved weight, BMI, LDL, triglycerides, glucose, blood pressure, and insulin resistance over 84 days.","direct_quote":"Weight, BMI, total cholesterol, LDL, triglycerides, fasting glucose, systolic and diastolic blood pressure, insulin resistance and fasting insulin were significantly improved after IF","fact_category":"causal_link","relevance_score":0.93,"confidence":0.92,"perspective":"Public_Health","entities":["16+8 TRF","metabolic syndrome","LDL","blood pressure"]},{"statement":"In McNeil et al. 2014, Ramadan fasting in normal-weight and obese men significantly increased glucose, total cholesterol, and LDL-C levels during the fasting period.","direct_quote":"significant increased of glucose, total cholesterol and LDL-C levels during Ramadan fast in normal-weight and obese men","fact_category":"statistic","relevance_score":0.86,"confidence":0.88,"perspective":"Public_Health","entities":["Ramadan fasting","glucose","LDL-C","total cholesterol"]}],"evidence_summary":"Individual RCT and CRS findings from the meta-analysis table, documenting protocol-specific effects of ADF, TRF, and Ramadan fasting on metabolic markers."}]}
